div1.entryFree: Detect, to discover, to find out, to betray: I will d. my wife, Wiv. II, 2, 325. to be --ed with a jealous bell-wether, III, 5, 111 (with == by). I never heard the absent duke much --ed for women, Meas. III, 2, 130. groaning every hour would d. the lazy foot of time, As III, 2, 322. to let thy tongue d. thy base-born heart, H6C II, 2, 143. R3 I, 4, 141. Tit. II, 4, 27. Hml. III, 2, 94. Per. II, 1, 55.
